
```{r}

################################################################################
# PROJECT: Avian erythropoiesis reveals mechanisms of nuclear retention and the ontogeny of erythrocyte immunity
# SCRIPT:  02_Integration_Avian_Erythroid_Atlas_PeripheralBlood.R
# 
# PURPOSE: Integration of mature erythrocytes and bone-marrow to peripheral-blood 
#          trajectory from public dataset (PBscRNA).
#
# RATIONALE: While marrow-derived (BMscRNA) data captures early progenitor 
#            through late blast stages, it lacks a significant population of 
#            fully mature erythrocytes, which enter circulation upon final 
#            maturation. This script incorporates peripheral blood data to 
#            bridge the gap and complete the single-cell trajectory.
################################################################################

# --- 1. Load All Required Libraries ---
library(Seurat)      # Single-cell RNA-seq analysis
library(ggplot2)     # Plotting and themes
library(dplyr)       # Data manipulation
library(cowplot)     # Arranging multi-panel plots
library(patchwork)   # Plot combining
library(scMetabolism)# Metabolic pathway analysis
library(RColorBrewer)# Color palettes
library(tidyr)       # Data cleaning
library(plyr)        # Mapping values

# --- 2. Directory Setup ---
# Set working directory to the project root
# setwd("path/to/repository") 

if (!dir.exists("./Results")) dir.create("./Results")
if (!dir.exists("./Processed_Objects")) dir.create("./Processed_Objects")
if (!dir.exists("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files")) {
  dir.create("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files", recursive = TRUE)
}

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 2A: LOAD & PROCESS PUBLIC PERIPHERAL BLOOD DATA (PBscRNA)
# ==============================================================================

# 3. Load Raw 10X Genomics Data (Maxwell et al. 2024)
# NOTE:  Download the folders 'Sample_1' through 'Sample_4' from the GitHub 
# link: https://github.com/maxwelma/exjobb/tree/main/Data
# Place them locally into respective folders: ./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/

sample1 <- ReadMtx(
  mtx = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_1/matrix.mtx",
  features = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_1/features.tsv",
  cells = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_1/barcodes.tsv"
)
sample2 <- ReadMtx(
  mtx = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_2/matrix.mtx",
  features = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_2/features.tsv",
  cells = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_2/barcodes.tsv"
)
sample3 <- ReadMtx(
  mtx = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_3/matrix.mtx",
  features = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_3/features.tsv",
  cells = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_3/barcodes.tsv"
)
sample4 <- ReadMtx(
  mtx = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_4/matrix.mtx",
  features = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_4/features.tsv",
  cells = "./Raw_Data/Public_Reference_Data/Maxwell_2024_PBscRNA/PBscRNA_4/barcodes.tsv"
)

#Individual objects are created first to allow for project labeling before merging into a unified Peripheral Blood (PB) object.
sample1_seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = sample1, project = "Sample1")
sample2_seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = sample2, project = "Sample2")
sample3_seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = sample3, project = "Sample3")
sample4_seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = sample4, project = "Sample4")

# Merge biological replicates for PB-specific processing
PBscRNA <- merge(sample1_seurat, y = list(sample2_seurat, sample3_seurat, sample4_seurat),
                    add.cell.ids = c("S1", "S2", "S3", "S4"), project = "PBscRNA", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)

# 4. Quality Control Calculation
# NOTE: Mitochondrial genes are absent in GSE224329 annotations. 
# We utilize percent_ribo and percent_hb as the primary biological QC metrics.

# Open a PDF device to save plots
pdf(file = "./Results/Supplementary Figure 2-A.pdf", width = 12, height = 8)
PBscRNA <- PercentageFeatureSet(PBscRNA, "^RP[SL]", col.name = "percent_ribo")
PBscRNA <- PercentageFeatureSet(PBscRNA, "^HB[^(P)]", col.name = "percent_hb")
PBscRNA <- PercentageFeatureSet(PBscRNA, "PECAM1|PF4", col.name = "percent_plat")

# Plot violin plots safely (avoid patchwork bug in Seurat v5)
vln_list <- lapply(
  c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent_ribo", "percent_hb", "percent_plat"),
  function(f) VlnPlot(PBscRNA, features = f, pt.size = 0.1) + ggtitle(f)
)
# Combine plots using cowplot
p_before <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = vln_list, ncol = 3)
print(p_before)
dev.off()

# Open a PDF device to save plots
pdf(file = "./Results/Supplementary Figure 2-B.pdf", width = 12, height = 8)


# 5. Apply Filtering Criteria
#  Based on the study's protocol, we exclude low-quality cells 
# (nFeature < 200, nCount < 500) and high-feature multiplets (> 6000).
PBscRNA <- subset(
  PBscRNA,
  subset = nFeature_RNA > 200 & nFeature_RNA < 6000 &
            nCount_RNA > 500 & nCount_RNA < 10000 &
            percent_ribo < 10 & percent_plat < 10
)
# Plot violin plots safely (avoid patchwork bug in Seurat v5)
vln_list2 <- lapply(
  c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent_ribo", "percent_hb", "percent_plat"),
  function(f) VlnPlot(PBscRNA, features = f, pt.size = 0.1) + ggtitle(f)
)
# Combine plots using cowplot
p_after <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = vln_list2, ncol = 3)
print(p_after)
dev.off()

# 6. Normalization and Clustering
# We use the top 20 PCs for clustering to identify the "Mature Erythrocyte" population.
PBscRNA <- NormalizeData(PBscRNA)
PBscRNA <- FindVariableFeatures(PBscRNA, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)
# Scale the data
PBscRNA <- ScaleData(PBscRNA, features = VariableFeatures(PBscRNA))
#Perform PCA
PBscRNA <- RunPCA(PBscRNA, features = VariableFeatures(object = PBscRNA))
# PCA plot by orig.ident
pdf("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Before_Integration.pdf", width = 4, height = 4)
DimPlot(PBscRNA, reduction = "pca", group.by = "orig.ident", pt.size = 0.5, cols = c("green", "red", "black", "yellow", "orange", "pink", "blue"))
dev.off()
# Elbow plot for PCA
pdf("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/PBscRNA_Elbow_Plot.pdf", width = 6, height = 4.5)
ElbowPlot(PBscRNA, ndims = 50, reduction = "pca")
dev.off()
#Cluster the Cells
PBscRNA <- FindNeighbors(PBscRNA, dims = 1:20)
PBscRNA <- FindClusters(PBscRNA, resolution = 0.5)
# UMAP plot
pdf("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/PBscRNA-TSNE.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
# Visualize clusters
DimPlot(PBscRNA, reduction = "pca", label = TRUE)
dev.off()
#Run UMAP/t-SNE for Visualization
set.seed(1)
PBscRNA <- RunUMAP(PBscRNA, dims = 1:20)
# UMAP plot
pdf("./Results/Supplementary Figure 2-C.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
# Plot UMAP
set.seed(1)
DimPlot(PBscRNA, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)
dev.off()

# Save the processed object under the new name
save(PBscRNA, file = "./Processed_Objects/PBscRNA_Processed.Rdata")
# The 'PBscRNA_Processed.Rdata' object serves as the definitive reference for 
# terminal maturation in this study. Cluster 5 (Mature Erythrocytes) will be 
# validated via Pearson correlation in SCRIPT 2B and subsequently isolated 
# in SCRIPT 2C for integration with the bone-marrow trajectory (BMscRNA) 
# to bridge the gap between erythroid blasts and terminal maturity.


# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 2B: CROSS-DATASET BENCHMARKING & MATURITY VALIDATION
# ==============================================================================

# 7. Marker Identification for PBscRNA Atlas
# JUSTIFICATION: Before integrating with bone marrow, we identify cluster-specific 
# markers in the peripheral blood dataset to confirm that Cluster 5 exhibits 
# high hemoglobin expression and terminal erythroid signatures.
load(file = "./Processed_Objects/PBscRNA_Processed.Rdata")

# RATIONALE: JoinLayers is used in Seurat v5 to consolidate count matrices 
# across replicates for consistent marker identification.
PBscRNA <- JoinLayers(PBscRNA)
# Now, identify DEGs for each cluster
all_markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  PBscRNA,
  only.pos = TRUE,  # Finds only upregulated (positive) markers
  min.pct = 0.25,   # Only test genes that are expressed in at least 25% of cells
  logfc.threshold = 0.25  # Minimum log fold-change threshold
)
# Save top 20 genes per cluster for manuscript documentation
top20 <- all_markers %>% group_by(cluster) %>% top_n(n=20, wt = avg_log2FC)
write.csv(top20, file = "./Results/Supplementary Data 4.csv")


# 8. Cross-Dataset Correlation Analysis (BM vs PB)
# PURPOSE: To quantitatively prove that PB Cluster 5 represents the terminal 
# biological successor to the BM 'Immature Erythrocyte' stage.
load(file = "./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_Processed.Rdata")

# Dictionary mapping BM numeric clusters to their biological identities
cluster_annotations <- c(
  "8" = "Immature erythrocyte",
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast",
  "4" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "1" = "Early polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast",
  "6" = "Early erythroblast",
  "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)

#AverageExpression collapses single-cell noise into a stable 
# "pseudobulk" profile. Pearson correlation on these profiles is the gold 
# standard for verifying developmental equivalence across different datasets.
avg_exp_pb <- AverageExpression(PBscRNA, assays = "RNA", layer = "data")$RNA
avg_exp_bm <- AverageExpression(BMscRNA, assays = "RNA", layer = "data")$RNA

# Ensure a fair comparison by using only genes common to both datasets
common_genes <- intersect(rownames(avg_exp_pb), rownames(avg_exp_bm))

# Isolate PB Cluster 5 (Target) and the Bone Marrow erythroid stages (Reference)
pb_target_cluster_g <- "g5" 
# Get numeric IDs from our annotation map and add the 'g' prefix
bm_target_clusters_g <- paste0("g", names(cluster_annotations))

# Robustness check: Ensure all target clusters exist in the data
if (!pb_target_cluster_g %in% colnames(avg_exp_pb)) {
  stop(paste("Error: Target cluster", pb_target_cluster_g, "not found in PBscRNA."))
}
if (!all(bm_target_clusters_g %in% colnames(avg_exp_bm))) {
  stop(paste("Error: One or more target clusters not found in BMscRNA."))
}

# Prepare data and calculate correlation
pb_cluster_vec   <- as.matrix(avg_exp_pb[common_genes, pb_target_cluster_g, drop = FALSE])
bm_target_matrix <- as.matrix(avg_exp_bm[common_genes, bm_target_clusters_g])
correlation_results <- cor(pb_cluster_vec, bm_target_matrix, method = "pearson")
correlation_vector <- correlation_results[1, ]


# 9. Correlation Visualization 
# JUSTIFICATION: High correlation values (>0.90) with the 'Immature Erythrocyte' 
# cluster provide the biological justification for integrating these cells 
# as the terminal maturation point.

# --- Prepare Data Frame for Plotting ---
sorted_correlations <- sort(correlation_vector, decreasing = TRUE)
# Get the programmatic names ("g8", "g0") and clean them to numeric IDs ("8", "0")
numeric_ids_sorted <- gsub("^g", "", names(sorted_correlations))
correlation_df <- data.frame(
  Annotation = cluster_annotations[numeric_ids_sorted],
  Correlation = sorted_correlations
)
# Ensure the plot order is based on correlation value, not alphabetical annotation
correlation_df$Annotation <- factor(correlation_df$Annotation, levels = correlation_df$Annotation)
annotated_plot <- ggplot(correlation_df, aes(x = Annotation, y = Correlation, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Correlation, 3)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", name = "Correlation") +

  labs(
    title = "Correlation of PBscRNA Cluster 5 with Annotated BMscRNA Clusters",
    x = "BMscRNA Cluster Annotation",
    y = "Pearson Correlation Coefficient"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)) 
  )
# Show the plot in your R session
print(annotated_plot)

#Save the Final Annotated Plot as a PDF
ggsave(
  "./Results/Figure-2B.pdf",
  plot = annotated_plot,
  width = 10, 
  height = 8
)
# The high correlation confirmed in SCRIPT 2B validates the use of PB Cluster 5 
# as the terminal 'Mature Erythrocyte'. We now proceed to SCRIPT 2C to 
# perform the final batch-corrected integration of marrow and blood datasets.

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 2C: INTEGRATED AVIAN ERYTHROID ATLAS CONSTRUCTION
# ==============================================================================

# 10. Dataset Subsetting & Preparation
# To create a clean maturation trajectory, we isolate the 
# validated terminal stage (PB Cluster 5) and the marrow developmental stages. 
# This removes non-erythroid "noise" from the final integration.

# Assuming you've already loaded datasets BMscRNA and PBscRNA & have done its characterization of clusters
# Set default assays and identifiers
DefaultAssay(BMscRNA) <- "RNA"  # Use RNA for integration
Idents(PBscRNA) <- "seurat_clusters"

# Isolate the Mature Erythrocytes from PB
# We rename the identity to '9' to ensure it remains distinct 
# from the marrow clusters ("8", "0", "4", "1", "2", "6", "5", "3") in the integrated object.
mature_cluster <- subset(PBscRNA, idents = "5")  # Mature erythrocyte
mature_cluster <- RenameIdents(mature_cluster, `5` = "9")

# Isolate the Developmental Stages from Bone Marrow
# Using the clusters identified in Script 01
dev_stages <- subset(BMscRNA, idents = c("8", "0", "4", "1", "2", "6", "5", "3"))  # Developmental stages

# 11. Anchor-Based Integration
# JUSTIFICATION: Marrow and blood datasets represent different biological 
# compartments and technical batches. FindIntegrationAnchors identifies 
# shared biological states to harmonize the data into a single manifold.
# Merge datasets
combined <- merge(mature_cluster, y = dev_stages, add.cell.ids = c("Blood", "BoneMarrow"))
# Set the RNA assay for further processing
DefaultAssay(combined) <- "RNA"
# Normalize and scale data
combined <- NormalizeData(combined, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 10000)
combined <- ScaleData(combined, verbose = FALSE)
# Run PCA
combined <- RunPCA(combined, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)
# Find integration anchors and integrate data
anchors <- FindIntegrationAnchors(object.list = list(mature_cluster, dev_stages), dims = 1:50)
combined <- IntegrateData(anchorset = anchors)

# 12. Integrated Data Processing
# Dimensionality reduction and visualize the developmental bridge between marrow and blood.
# Scale data and perform PCA
combined <- ScaleData(combined, verbose = FALSE)
combined <- RunPCA(combined, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)

# Elbow plot for Integrated Object
set.seed(155)
pdf("./Results/Supplementary Figure 2-E.pdf")
ElbowPlot(combined, ndims = 50)
dev.off()

# Clustering and UMAP
combined <- RunUMAP(combined, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:20)

# Generate UMAP plot colored by 'orig.ident'
umap_plot <- DimPlot(combined, reduction = "umap", group.by = "orig.ident", label = TRUE) +
  ggtitle("UMAP Plot Colored by orig.ident")
pdf(file = "./Results/Supplementary Figure 2-D.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)  # Specify the PDF file name and dimensions
print(umap_plot)
dev.off()  # Close the PDF device

# PCA plot by orig.ident
pdf("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Orig.Ident_Joined.pdf", width = 4, height = 4)
DimPlot(combined, reduction = "pca", group.by = "orig.ident", pt.size = 0.5, cols = c("green", "red", "black", "yellow", "orange", "pink", "blue"))
dev.off()

# Find neighbors and clusters
combined <- FindNeighbors(combined, dims = 1:20)
combined <- FindClusters(combined, resolution = 0.5)

# UMAP plot
pdf("./Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Tsne_after_first_findneighbours.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
DimPlot(combined, reduction = "pca", label = TRUE)
dev.off()

# 13. High-Level Annotation & Cleanup
# We define the two primary biological groups (Mature vs Developmental) 
# and remove any residual "Other" clusters to produce the final Erythroid Atlas.
combined$cell_type <- "Other"
combined$cell_type[combined$seurat_clusters %in% "9"] <- "Mature erythrocyte"
combined$cell_type[combined$seurat_clusters %in% c("8", "0", "4", "1", "2", "6", "5", "3")] <- "Developmental Stages"
# Remove the "Other" category
BMscRNA_PBscRNA <- subset(combined, cell_type != "Other")
# Update the metadata with the cell type information
BMscRNA_PBscRNA$cell_type <- as.factor(BMscRNA_PBscRNA$cell_type)
# Run UMAP for filtered combined data
BMscRNA_PBscRNA <- RunUMAP(BMscRNA_PBscRNA, dims = 1:20)

# 14. Visualization of Integrated Trajectory 
# This plot represents the cornerstone of the integrated atlas, 
# demonstrating the transition from marrow-resident blasts to terminal maturity.
set.seed(172)
pdf("./Results/Figure-2A.pdf")
DimPlot(BMscRNA_PBscRNA, group.by = "cell_type", label = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "red", "grey")) +
  ggtitle("UMAP: Mature erythrocytes vs Developmental Stages")
dev.off()

# 15. Save Final Integrated Object
# NOTE: This object serves as the input for all terminal gene expression 
# and metabolic persistence analyses.
saveRDS(BMscRNA_PBscRNA, file = "./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")


# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 2D: MARKER EXPRESSION & METABOLIC PROFILING
# ==============================================================================

# 16. Integrated Marker Discovery
# We identify cluster-specific markers across the full integrated lineage.

# We use readRDS for consistent object loading and memory efficiency.
BMscRNA_PBscRNA <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")

# These markers serve as the molecular fingerprints for each developmental stage defined in our avian erythroid atlas.
all_markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  BMscRNA_PBscRNA,
  only.pos = TRUE, 
  min.pct = 0.25,   
  logfc.threshold = 0.25  
)
# Save top 20 genes per cluster for manuscript documentation
top20 <- all_markers %>% group_by(cluster) %>% top_n(n=20, wt = avg_log2FC)
write.csv(top20, file = "/Results/Result_Supporting_Files/Integrated_Erythroid_Markers.csv")

# 17.Marker gene expression visualization set assay and identities
DefaultAssay(BMscRNA_PBscRNA) <- "SCT"
Idents(BMscRNA_PBscRNA) <- "seurat_clusters"

# Define cluster name mapping for the integrated lineage
new_cluster_names <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast",
  "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast",
  "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "4" = "Early polychromatic blast",
  "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte",
  "8" = "Early erythroblast",
  "9" = "Mature erythrocyte"
)
# Rename cluster identities directly using Idents
BMscRNA_PBscRNA <- RenameIdents(BMscRNA_PBscRNA, new_cluster_names)
#Setting explicit factor levels ensures the DotPlot reflects the 
# biological maturation order (Mature Erythrocyte to Erythroid Progenitor 1).
new_order <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte", 
  "Immature erythrocyte", 
  "Late polychromatic blast", 
  "Mid polychromatic blast", 
  "Early polychromatic blast", 
  "Late erythroblast", 
  "Early erythroblast", 
  "Erythroid progenitor 2", 
  "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)
# Set factor levels explicitly
BMscRNA_PBscRNA@active.ident <- factor(Idents(BMscRNA_PBscRNA), levels = new_order)
# Curated terminal erythroid markers
marker_genes_red <- c("ENSGALG00010001729", "RFESD", "CA13", 
                      "HBA1", "HBAD", "HBBA", "HBE1", "EPAS1", "CA2",
                      "PPOX", "FECH", "PON1",
                      "CHST2", "COQ9", "MPST", "MXD3")
# Filter to keep only those genes present in the dataset
marker_genes_present <- marker_genes_red[marker_genes_red %in% rownames(BMscRNA_PBscRNA)]

# 18. Lineage Maturation Signatures 
DefaultAssay(BMscRNA_PBscRNA) <- "RNA"
Idents(BMscRNA_PBscRNA) <- "seurat_clusters"
genes_to_plot <- c(
  "HBA1", "HBBA", "HBAD", "HBE1", "CA2", "FTH1",
  "IFRD1", "NCOA4", "TFRC", "ARIH1", "H1F0", "H3F3C",
  "H2AFZ", "SPTAN1", "EPB41", "HDAC2"
)
genes_to_plot_present <- genes_to_plot[genes_to_plot %in% rownames(BMscRNA_PBscRNA)]
cat("Genes present in dataset:\n")
print(genes_to_plot_present)

# Generate individual violin plots
violinplot_file <- "./Results/Figure-2C.pdf"
violin_list <- VlnPlot(
  BMscRNA_PBscRNA,
  features = genes_to_plot_present,
  pt.size = 0.3,
  combine = FALSE
)

violin_list <- lapply(
  violin_list,
  function(p) p +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      axis.title.x = element_blank(),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "bold")
    )
)

violin_combined <- wrap_plots(violin_list, ncol = 4) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Violin Plot for Selected Genes")
ggsave(filename = violinplot_file, plot = violin_combined, width = 14, height = 8)
cat("✅ Combined violin plot saved successfully to:", violinplot_file, "\n")

# 19. Metabolism Dataset Preparation

#We reload the integrated RDS object to ensure a clean starting point.
chicken <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")

#To ensure the metabolic DotPlot reflects the biological maturation timeline,we explicitly rename and re-order the clusters here. 
# This ensures "Mature" cells and "Progenitors" are grouped logically on the X-axis.
new_cluster_names_chicken <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast", "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast", "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "4" = "Early polychromatic blast", "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte", "8" = "Early erythroblast",
  "9" = "Mature erythrocyte"
)

# Define the custom order for chicken clusters
custom_order_chicken <- rev(c(
  "Mature erythrocyte", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast", 
  "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast", 
  "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1"
))

# Apply the renaming and reordering
chicken$custom_cluster_names <- plyr::mapvalues(
  x = chicken$seurat_clusters,
  from = names(new_cluster_names_chicken),
  to = unname(new_cluster_names_chicken),
  warn_missing = TRUE
)
chicken$custom_cluster_names <- factor(chicken$custom_cluster_names, levels = custom_order_chicken)
Idents(chicken) <- "custom_cluster_names"
cat("--- Table of renamed and reordered cluster identities ---\n")
print(table(Idents(chicken)))
cat("\n--- Data Overview ---\n")
print(chicken)
cat("\nAvailable assays:", names(chicken@assays), "\n")
cat("Gene names in RNA assay look like this:", head(rownames(chicken[['RNA']])), "\n")

# 20. scMetabolism Analysis Workflow
# scMetabolism requires a specific data structure. 
# We create a subset containing only cells from the SCT assay to minimize 
# technical variance and ensure normalized counts are used for AUCell scoring.
cat("\n\n--- Starting scMetabolism Analysis ---\n")
cat("Creating a subset of the Seurat object with only cells from the SCT assay...\n")
cells_in_sct <- colnames(chicken[["SCT"]])
chicken_sct_subset <- subset(chicken, cells = cells_in_sct)
DefaultAssay(chicken_sct_subset) <- "SCT"
cat("Verification of 'chicken_sct_subset' successful:\n")
print(chicken_sct_subset)

#We create a temporary simplified object to ensure compatibility between Seurat v5 layer structures and the scMetabolism package.
cat("\nPreparing a temporary object for scMetabolism function...\n")
sct_counts_matrix <- LayerData(chicken_sct_subset, assay = "SCT", layer = "counts")
sct_data_matrix <- LayerData(chicken_sct_subset, assay = "SCT", layer = "data")
sct_metadata <- chicken_sct_subset@meta.data
temp_sct_assay <- CreateAssayObject(counts = sct_counts_matrix)
LayerData(temp_sct_assay, layer = "data") <- sct_data_matrix
temp_seurat_obj <- CreateSeuratObject(
    counts = temp_sct_assay,
    meta.data = sct_metadata
)
temp_seurat_obj$custom_cluster_names <- chicken_sct_subset$custom_cluster_names

# Run scMetabolism
chicken_metabolism_output <- NULL
tryCatch({
    cat("Running sc.metabolism.Seurat (this may take a few minutes)...\n")
    chicken_metabolism_output <- sc.metabolism.Seurat(
      obj = temp_seurat_obj,
      method = "AUCell",
      imputation = FALSE,
      ncores = 2,
      metabolism.type = "KEGG"
    )
    cat("sc.metabolism.Seurat completed successfully.\n")
}, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error during sc.metabolism.Seurat:", e$message, "\n")
})

# 21. Transfer & Verification of Results
# We move the calculated metabolism scores back to our primary Seurat subset to enable standardized plotting.
if (!is.null(chicken_metabolism_output) && "METABOLISM" %in% names(chicken_metabolism_output@assays)) {
    cat("Transferring metabolism scores back to the main subset object...\n")
    
    metabolism_scores_matrix <- as.matrix(chicken_metabolism_output@assays$METABOLISM$score)
    
    original_cell_names <- colnames(chicken_sct_subset)
    standardized_cell_names_for_matching <- gsub(pattern = "-", replacement = ".", x = original_cell_names)
    
    if (ncol(metabolism_scores_matrix) == length(standardized_cell_names_for_matching)) {
        metabolism_scores_matrix <- metabolism_scores_matrix[, standardized_cell_names_for_matching, drop = FALSE]
        colnames(metabolism_scores_matrix) <- original_cell_names
        
        chicken_sct_subset[["METABOLISM"]] <- CreateAssayObject(data = metabolism_scores_matrix)
        DefaultAssay(chicken_sct_subset) <- "METABOLISM"
        
        cat("METABOLISM assay added successfully.\n")
    } else {
        stop("Mismatch in cell numbers between scMetabolism output and input Seurat object. Cannot proceed.")
    }
} else {
  stop("scMetabolism failed or did not produce the expected 'METABOLISM' assay.")
}

# 22. Visualization of Metabolic Trajectory 
# A DotPlot representing activity across key metabolic pathways 
cat("\n\n--- Generating Visualizations (Updated Style) ---\n")
# Verify pathways calculated
all_calculated_pathways <- rownames(chicken_sct_subset[["METABOLISM"]])
cat("Total pathways scored:", length(all_calculated_pathways), "\n")
pat <- c(
  "Sulfur metabolism",
  "Starch and sucrose metabolism",
  "Sphingolipid metabolism",
  "Pyrimidine metabolism",
  "Purine metabolism",
  "Propanoate metabolism",
  "Porphyrin and chlorophyll metabolism",
  "Oxidative phosphorylation",
  "One carbon pool by folate",
  "Nitrogen metabolism",
  "Glyoxylate and dicarboxylate metabolism",
  "Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis",
  "Glutathione metabolism",
  "Galactose metabolism",
  "Arginine and proline metabolism"
)
# Define cluster names
cluster_names <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast",
  "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast",
  "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "4" = "Early polychromatic blast",
  "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte",
  "8" = "Early erythroblast",
  "9" = "Mature erythrocyte"
)

pdf(file.path("./Results/Figure 3B.pdf"), width = 10, height = 5)

DotPlot.metabolism(
  obj = chicken_metabolism_output,
  pathway = pat,
  phenotype = "custom_cluster_names",   
  norm = "y"
) +
  scale_x_discrete(
      limits = custom_order_chicken,     
      labels = custom_order_chicken
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  ) +
  ggtitle("Metabolic Pathway Activity across Chicken Erythroid Clusters")
dev.off()
cat("\n--- Figure-3B.pdf saved successfully ---\n")

```
